content.full_text:
EXECUTIVE OFFICE
1800 CITY HALL EAST
LOS ANGELES 90012
(213) 485-5408

CRIMINAL BRANCH
(213) 485-5470

CIVIL BRANCH
(213) 485-6370

TELECOPIER
(213) 660-3634

Office of the City Attorney
Los Angeles, California

JAMES K. HAHN
CITY ATTORNEY

March 20, 1990


Ms. Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Suite 800
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Ms. Donovan:

     Enclosed please find a letter from Los Angeles City
Councilman John Ferraro to our Office.  This letter sets forth
specific facts regarding a proposed agreement between the
Councilman and Assemblyman Mike Roos to pay for, and disseminate
throughout Mr. Roos' Assembly District, an annual birthday
greeting.  Pursuant to the facts contained in the attached letter,
Councilman Ferraro requests an opinion as to:

     ". . . whether or not it is legal for campaign
     funds contributed to [him] for [his]
     reelection be used in a way that directly
     benefits another elected official?"

     On March 8, 1990, I called the Fair Political Practices
Commission and spoke with Mr. Wayne Imbri.  I asked him if the
proposed agreement (as set forth in the attached letter) between
the Councilman and Assemblyman would violate any provisions of the
Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended on January 1, 1990.
Mr. Imbri informed me that this situation raises a number of
complex issues, which could not be answered informally or without
extensive research.  Thus, he advised me to send the Councilman's
inquiry to your attention and request your Commission to issue an
opinion regarding this situation.

     Hence, with the approval of the Councilman, I am
forwarding you a copy of the Councilman's letter and request the
Commission to render an opinion as to the legality of the proposed
agreement between the Councilman and Assemblyman.

Recyclable and made from recycled waste

Ms. Kathryn Donovan
March 20, 1990
Page 2


          If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate
to call me at the telephone number listed below.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        JAMES K. HAHN, City Attorney


                                        By    [signature]
                                             RAYMOND S. ILGUNAS
                                             Deputy City Attorney

RI:pe
(213) 237-0504

Attachment

cc:  Patricia Mount, Chief Deputy
     for Councilman John Ferraro

CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF LOS ANGELES
OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT

JOHN FERRARO
COUNCILMAN 4TH DISTRICT

M-30, CITY HALL
LOS ANGELES, CA 90012
(213) 485-3337

February 26, 1990

CITY ATTORNEY
LAND USE/ENVIRONMENT
RECEIVED
FEB 28 1990

Mr. Anthony S. Alperin
Office of the City Attorney
Room 1800, City Hall East

Dear Mr. Alperin:

Assemblyman Mike Roos sends an annual birthday greeting to all
constituents in his Assembly District. As portions of our
respective Districts overlap, he has suggested we might issue a
one-piece birthday card from the two of us.

On one side, the card would read "Best Wishes On Your Birthday"
with a Roos/Ferraro picture identifying Mike as Speaker Pro
Tempore of the California State Assembly and me as President of
the Los Angeles City Council. On the other side of the card
would be the name and address of the recipient.

The two campaign committees would split the cost of the cards
sent in overlapping areas. The Roos Committee would pay for the
portion in his area only and the Ferraro Committee would pay for
the cards mailed in my District.

My question is whether or not it is legal for campaign funds
contributed to me for my re-election be used in a way that
directly benefits another elected official?

I will appreciate your prompt response to my inquiry.

Sincerely,

JOHN FERRARO
Councilman, 4th District

JF:mmp

A COMPOSITE OF THE CITY OF LOS ANGELES

[The rest of the page appears to be blank or contains only very faint/illegible marks]

sections.question: whether or not it is legal for campaign funds contributed to [him] for [his] reelection be used in a way that directly benefits another elected official?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign funds contributed to Councilman Ferraro for his reelection can legally be used to fund a joint birthday greeting card with Assemblyman Mike Roos that would benefit another elected official.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC from the City Attorney's office, forwarding Councilman Ferraro's inquiry. The actual question is embedded in the attached councilman's letter dated February 26, 1990. No FPPC response or conclusion is present in this document. The document shows the City Attorney noted that FPPC staff (Wayne Imbri) advised extensive research was needed before an informal answer could be provided.

embedding.summary: Los Angeles City Attorney James K. Hahn forwards a request from Councilman John Ferraro to the FPPC seeking an advisory opinion on whether campaign funds can be used to jointly fund and distribute birthday greeting cards with Assemblyman Mike Roos across overlapping districts.
